fields.description:
Enter your Telegram Chat ID.
If you don't know it:
1. Just enter your Telegram Username.
2. Send a message to your Telegram Bot, and the system will automatically fetch and save your Chat ID.
fields.label: Chat ID

fields.description:
Enter a single-line Python expression to evaluate as a condition (must return True or False).

Available Variables:
• doc.field_name - Any field from the document (e.g., doc.grand_total)
• user - Email of the current session user (e.g., manager@example.com)
• current_date - System date of today (e.g., 2026-06-07)

Rules:
• ⚠️ Strict Rule: The condition must be written entirely on one single line.
• You can combine multiple rules using and / or operators.

Examples:
• doc.grand_total > 1000
• user == "ceo@example.com" and doc.status == "Submitted"
• doc.delivery_date == current_date
fields.label: Condition

fields.description:
Type your message template here using Jinja2 template syntax. You can inject dynamic variables by wrapping them in double curly braces {{ ... }}.

Available Variables & Examples:
• {{ doc.field_name }} - Any field from the current document (e.g., {{ doc.name }}, {{ doc.grand_total }})
• {{ user }} - ID/Email of the current session user (e.g., rami@example.com)
• {{ current_date }} - System date of today (e.g., 2026-06-09)

Full Example:
"Hi {{ user }}, Invoice {{ doc.name }} with items [{{ doc.items | map(attribute='item_code') | list | join(', ') }}] has been processed successfully on {{ current_date }}."
fields.label: Message
